name: DyadCore Multi-Session Preference Revision Benchmark
description: Evaluates memory systems on multi-session preference tracking, temporal reasoning, and knowledge update across field-specific decay half-lives.

scenarios.name: Tech Stack 30d (project_tech)
scenarios.description: Tests time decay ranking of old vs new technical preferences across 30-day gap

messages.content: I use VSCode as my main editor with lots of extensions for TypeScript and React.
VSCode has excellent TypeScript support. The extension ecosystem is unmatched.
Switched completely to Neovim. Much faster startup and I can configure everything in Lua.
Neovim's Lua config is powerful. Many devs are making that switch for performance.

questions.question: What code editor does the user currently use?
What editor did the user use before switching?
Why did the user switch editors?

scenarios.name: 饮食偏好 120d (personal_info)
scenarios.description: Tests long-gap (120d) preference revision on personal health/diet choices

messages.content: I drink 2-3 espressos daily. Love the deep dark roast bitterness.
Espresso is a classic. The dark roast gives you that bold flavor profile.
I love spicy food, especially Sichuan hotpot. The spicier the better!
Sichuan cuisine has that signature numbing heat. It's addictive.
After my health checkup, switched to decaf and herbal tea. No more espresso.
Decaf and herbal teas are much gentler on your system. Good health choice.
Developed stomach issues so now I prefer mild Cantonese steamed dishes and Japanese food.
Cantonese cuisine focuses on fresh ingredients and gentle cooking. Much easier on digestion.

questions.question: What does the user drink now?
What cuisine does the user prefer now?
Why did the user change their diet?
options: (b) Health reasons — checkup and stomach issues

messages.content: Critical bug: Login page has infinite redirect loop on iOS Safari browser.
iOS Safari sometimes handles redirect chains differently. Need to investigate the auth flow.
The iOS Safari login redirect bug is now fixed and deployed to production.
The fix was a missing state parameter in the OAuth callback. Good catch.

questions.question: What is the current status of the iOS Safari login bug?
What was the original bug symptom?

scenarios.name: Sprint 5d (meeting_notes)
scenarios.description: Tests very-short-gap (5d) meeting note goal evolution

messages.content: Sprint planning decision: Q2 focus will be on performance optimization across all services.
Performance work is long overdue. We should target p95 latency reduction.
Performance optimization goals achieved. Now shifting focus to reliability engineering.
Reliability is the natural next step. SLO-based alerting and chaos engineering.

questions.question: What is the current sprint focus?
What was the previous sprint goal?

messages.content: Our deployment is all shell scripts. scp tarballs to servers and run setup.sh manually.
Shell scripts work for simple setups but become hard to maintain as you grow.
Moved from shell scripts to Docker Compose. Much more reproducible deployments now.
Docker Compose gives you environment consistency. Big improvement over shell scripts.
Now using Kubernetes with Helm charts. CI/CD via GitHub Actions pushes to our K8s cluster.
Kubernetes is the gold standard for container orchestration at scale.

questions.question: What is the user's CURRENT deployment method?
options: (c) Kubernetes with Helm and GitHub Actions

questions.question: What deployment method did the user use FIRST?
What did the user migrate FROM to get to Kubernetes?

scenarios.name: Anchor 冲突 (personal_info)
scenarios.description: Anchored old preference vs new pescatarian preference

messages.content: I'm a strict vegetarian. No meat, no fish — it's an ethical choice I've held for years.
That's a strong commitment. Vegetarianism for ethical reasons is admirable.
I drink Coke with every meal. It's my guilty pleasure.
Coke is a classic but that's a lot of sugar. Maybe try sparkling water sometimes.
Started eating fish occasionally. I'm pescatarian now — needed more protein options.
Pescatarian gives you omega-3s and lean protein. A practical middle ground.
Switched from Coke to just water and unsweetened tea. Feel much better.
Cutting out sugary drinks is one of the best health moves you can make.

questions.question: What is the user's current diet preference?
What was the user's previous diet?
What does the user drink with meals now?

scenarios.name: 跨场域隔离 — MySQL 检索隔离
scenarios.description: Same keyword in different fields — tests field isolation

messages.content: Our search feature uses MySQL LIKE queries for full-text search on product descriptions.
MySQL LIKE works for simple search but doesn't scale well. Consider full-text indexes.
I collect MySQL-related memorabilia. Got a vintage MySQL dolphin plushie from a 2006 conference.
That's a rare collector's item! The MySQL dolphin mascot has gone through several redesigns.
Search is now on Elasticsearch. We dropped MySQL LIKE queries entirely.
Elasticsearch is purpose-built for search. Much better relevance and performance.

questions.question: What search technology does the project currently use?
What personal item does the user own?

scenarios.name: 部分更新 — 仅后端 (project_tech)
scenarios.description: Partial stack update: only backend changes

messages.content: Our stack is Express.js backend with React frontend. REST API between them.
Express + React is a battle-tested combo. Simple and effective for most apps.
We rewrote the backend in FastAPI (Python). Frontend is still React — no changes there.
FastAPI gives you async performance and auto-generated OpenAPI docs. Frontend stays the same.

questions.question: What backend framework does the user currently use?
What frontend framework does the user use?

scenarios.name: 隐式漂移 — 无显式切换 (personal_info)
scenarios.description: Preference drift without explicit switch statements

messages.content: I watch Netflix every night after work. Binged all the top thrillers this year.
Netflix has an incredible thriller catalog. Hard to stop once you start bingeing.
Chopin's nocturnes are my favorite music. I listen while reading.
Chopin is timeless. The nocturnes create a perfect contemplative atmosphere.
Went to a classical music concert at the symphony hall last weekend. The live experience was transcendent.
Live classical performances have a depth that recordings can't fully capture.
Been getting into jazz lately. Miles Davis and John Coltrane are opening up a whole new world.
Jazz is an incredible journey. Davis and Coltrane are the perfect gateway artists.

questions.question: What entertainment does the user currently prefer?
options: (b) Live classical concerts and jazz music

questions.question: What music is the user currently into?
What was the user's previous evening activity?

scenarios.name: 超长衰减 365d (personal_info)
scenarios.description: 365-day decay test: old memory beyond half-life

messages.content: I'm a die-hard fiction reader. Thrillers and mysteries are my absolute favorites.
Thrillers keep you turning pages. The suspense craft in good mysteries is unmatched.
Now I mainly read nonfiction — behavioral economics and cognitive science. Much more practical.
Behavioral economics explains so much about how we actually make decisions vs how we think we do.

questions.question: What genre does the user currently read?
options: (b) Nonfiction — behavioral economics and cognitive science

questions.question: What was the user's previous reading preference?

scenarios.name: 噪声会话干扰 (project_tech, 14d + noise)
scenarios.description: Key preference info embedded in noisy conversations about unrelated topics

messages.content: Our main database is PostgreSQL with JSONB columns for flexible schemas.
PG JSONB is great for semi-structured data with full queryability.
Hey, did you watch the Champions League final last night? What a match!
I heard it was thrilling! Football brings people together like nothing else.
For monitoring we use Grafana dashboards hooked up to Prometheus metrics.
Grafana + Prometheus is the standard open-source observability stack.
I need to buy a birthday gift for my nephew. Any ideas for a 10-year-old?
LEGO sets or a beginner robotics kit are always hits with that age group.
We handle auth with JWT tokens stored in HTTP-only cookies, refresh every 15min.
JWT in HTTP-only cookies is secure. Short refresh windows limit exposure.
Switched from PostgreSQL JSONB to a dedicated MongoDB for flexible schema parts.
MongoDB is purpose-built for document stores. Good call for that use case.
Can you recommend a good sushi place in downtown? Taking a client out Friday.
Sushi Nakazawa is highly rated for business dinners. Omakase menu impresses.
We replaced Prometheus + Grafana with Datadog for better integrated monitoring.
Datadog costs more but the integrated APM + logs + metrics in one pane is worth it.
Auth is now session-based with Redis-backed session store, dropped JWT entirely.
Server-side sessions give you instant revocation. Important for security-sensitive apps.

questions.question: What database does the user currently use for flexible schemas?
What monitoring solution does the user use now?
How does the user handle authentication now?

scenarios.name: 多维度同步演化 (personal_info, 60d, 3 tracks)
scenarios.description: Three independent preferences (diet, exercise, media) evolve simultaneously

messages.content: I run 5K three times a week, training for a half-marathon later this year.
Consistent 5K runs build a great aerobic base for half-marathon training.
I mostly watch sci-fi series. Just finished The Expanse, amazing show.
The Expanse sets a high bar for sci-fi. Hard to top that world-building.
For breakfast I usually have cereal with milk and a banana. Quick and easy.
Simple breakfasts work. Carbs for energy and potassium from the banana.
Switched from running to swimming. Better for my knees and full-body workout.
Swimming is zero-impact and excellent cardio. Great choice for joint health.
Got into historical documentaries lately. Finished a great series on ancient Rome.
Historical documentaries can be just as gripping as fiction when well-produced.
Changed breakfast to Greek yogurt with granola and berries. More protein, less sugar.
Greek yogurt has double the protein of regular yogurt. Berries add antioxidants.

questions.question: What exercise does the user currently do?
What genre does the user currently watch?
What does the user eat for breakfast now?
options: (b) Greek yogurt with granola and berries

scenarios.name: 半衰期边界 bugs 6d vs 8d (half=7d)
scenarios.description: Two bugs at 6d and 8d — straddling the 7d half-life boundary

messages.content: Bug B-201: Dashboard charts not rendering on Firefox ESR, SVG transform issue.
SVG transform compatibility varies across browsers. Firefox ESR is conservative.
Bug B-202: Checkout page times out for orders with more than 15 line items.
Timeout on large orders suggests a database query optimization problem.
Both bugs B-201 (Firefox SVG) and B-202 (checkout timeout) are now fixed and deployed.
SVG fix was a CSS workaround. Checkout fix was a query index improvement.

questions.question: Which bug was reported EARLIER?
What is the status of both bugs?

scenarios.name: 半衰期边界 project_tech 55d vs 65d (half=60d)
scenarios.description: Two migrations straddling the 60d half-life: 55d (within) vs 65d (past)

messages.content: We used to host everything on Heroku. Simple but gets expensive at scale.
Heroku is great for MVPs and small teams. The managed experience saves DevOps time.
Moved from Heroku to AWS EC2 with Terraform for infrastructure-as-code.
Terraform + EC2 gives you control and cost efficiency at scale.
Now using AWS ECS Fargate instead of raw EC2. Serverless containers are simpler.
Fargate eliminates the EC2 instance management overhead. True pay-per-use.

questions.question: What infrastructure does the user CURRENTLY use?
What did the user use BEFORE EC2?
What infrastructure did the user migrate FROM to get to Fargate?

scenarios.name: 快速连续修正 (personal_info, 3d to 0d)
scenarios.description: Preference revises twice in 3 days. Only the latest counts.

messages.content: I want to learn guitar. Been listening to a lot of flamenco music lately.
Flamenco guitar is technically demanding but incredibly expressive.
Actually no, I think piano would be better. More versatile and easier to learn theory on.
Piano is the best instrument for learning music theory. All notes laid out linearly.
Changed my mind again. Going with drums. I just want to hit things and make rhythm!
Drums are pure rhythmic joy. Sometimes the simplest motivation is the best one.

questions.question: What instrument does the user want to learn NOW?
What was the user's FIRST choice of instrument?
Why did the user settle on drums?
options: (b) Wants to hit things and make rhythm
